summary_one_line: Input must contain a chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the PLTE FOURCC so chunk_name == png_PLTE holds true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG chunk whose 4-byte chunk-type field decodes to png_PLTE (ASCII 'PLTE' = 0x50 0x4c 0x54 0x45), making the equality test chunk_name == png_PLTE at pngread.c:159 evaluate true and dispatch png_handle_PLTE.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog resolves the branch in 10/10 trials and hits the true side 293 times on average; its I2SRandReplace mutator reads the failing 4-byte chunk_name comparison from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the png_PLTE constant directly into the chunk-type bytes, so a PLTE chunk appears in the input.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 10/10 trials with 0 hits on the true side; with only havoc/token mutators it never blindly lands the exact 4-byte PLTE tag at a valid chunk-type offset, so chunk_name == png_PLTE is never satisfied and execution falls through the else-if chain.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the integer CMP of chunk_name against the png_PLTE constant via __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp4, records both operands, and splices the constant operand into matching input bytes; naive has no CMP introspection and cannot target the exact magic tag.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocking branch is the equality test chunk_name == png_PLTE at pngread.c:159, true side globally blocked.
Taking the true side dispatches png_handle_PLTE, which only the winner reaches.
cmplog resolves the branch in all 10 trials and hits the true side 293 times on average while naive never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=293  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: naive blocks the branch in all 10 trials; cmplog resolves all 10.
The winner reaches the true side of line 159 while the loser never does (T=10 vs T=0).
cmplog's I2SRandReplace scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand, which lands magic constants like a chunk tag.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has only havoc/token mutators and no I2S, so it cannot introspect the chunk_name CMP.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3887 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 013b9f2a08a04135` and observed 1 I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 6 of the 7-level ancestor chain (ancestor 94c47bff5687a0c9), the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on chunk_name == png_PLTE where naive resolves the branch as fast as cmplog, showing the 4-byte magic-tag equality is reachable by blind havoc without CMP introspection.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte diff does not pinpoint a fixed PLTE-tag offset (PLTE chunks sit at variable offsets after IHDR/gAMA/sRGB), so the literal 'PLTE' bytes are not directly visible in the shown seed prefix; the FOURCC inference rests on the source equality and lineage I2S signal rather than a byte-level diff.
